task_completion.description: Visual Studio 2022 is open with the FinancialCalcTests.cs file active in the editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Visual Studio IDE is visible, and the editor tab clearly shows 'FinancialCalcTests.cs' as the active file.

task_completion.description: The test file contains at least three xUnit test methods for the LoanCalculator class.
task_completion.visual_evidence: C# code in the editor showing methods with [Fact] or [Theory] attributes that instantiate or call LoanCalculator methods, covering scenarios like happy path, zero interest, or negative principal.

task_completion.description: The test file contains at least three xUnit test methods for the CompoundInterestEngine class.
task_completion.visual_evidence: C# code in the editor showing methods with [Fact] or [Theory] attributes that instantiate or call CompoundInterestEngine methods, covering scenarios like future value or single payment.

task_completion.description: The test file contains at least three xUnit test methods for the CurrencyConverter class.
task_completion.visual_evidence: C# code in the editor showing methods with [Fact] or [Theory] attributes that instantiate or call CurrencyConverter methods, covering scenarios like direct conversions, multi-hop conversions, or invalid currency codes.

task_completion.description: The Visual Studio Test Explorer shows a minimum of 9 tests discovered or executed for the project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Test Explorer panel is visible in Visual Studio and lists at least 9 tests under the FinancialCalc.Tests project hierarchy (either passed, failed, or not run).

integrity.description: The agent used Visual Studio 2022 to write the tests.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the Visual Studio 2022 IDE interface being used to edit the code, rather than a basic text editor like Notepad.

integrity.description: The test methods contain actual assertions to verify behavior.
integrity.visual_evidence: The test code includes assertion statements (e.g., Assert.Equal, Assert.Throws, Assert.True) rather than being empty methods designed solely to inflate the test count.

integrity.description: Test discovery or execution results are genuine.
integrity.visual_evidence: Test results are displayed within the native Visual Studio Test Explorer or standard 'dotnet test' CLI output, not faked in a text file.
